decks.notes: This Dimir Tempo deck focuses on quickly disrupting the opponent's game plan while applying pressure with efficient creatures and spells. The deck aims to control the board through disruption like Thoughtseize and Force of Will, while also utilizing tempo-based cards like Daze to stay ahead. The deck's creatures like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Murktide Regent provide a clock for the opponent to deal with, while cards like Fatal Push and Orcish Bowmasters help to keep the board clear. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student adds card advantage and selection to help maintain the deck's tempo advantage.

The key strategy of this deck is to maintain control over the game by using counterspells, removal spells, and disruptive cards to prevent the opponent from establishing a strong board presence. The deck aims to control the game tempo and outvalue the opponent through card advantage and efficient answers to threats. By utilizing cards like Force of Will, Swords to Plowshares, and Teferi, Time Raveler, the deck seeks to disrupt the opponent's game plan while advancing its own win conditions, such as Quantum Riddler and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's resources and disrupting their game plan using cards like Wasteland, Swords to Plowshares, Stifle, and Force of Will. It also includes card advantage and selection tools like Ponder, Brainstorm, and Teferi, Time Raveler. Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provide further card advantage and potential win conditions. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent by efficiently managing resources and consistently answering threats while generating card advantage.

This Dimir Tempo deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan while efficiently deploying threats to apply pressure. It aims to control the board through a combination of hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize and efficient removal spells like Fatal Push. The deck also utilizes powerful threats like Murktide Regent and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares to close out the game quickly. The inclusion of counterspells like Force of Will and Daze helps protect your own game plan while disrupting the opponent's. Overall, the deck aims to tempo out opponents and maintain control of the game.

The deck aims to quickly mill through its entire library to win the game with the Thassa's Oracle or Dread Return combo. It uses a variety of fast mana sources and creatures to facilitate the combo, along with protection in the form of Pact of Negation. The deck also includes cards like Cabal Therapy and Memory's Journey to disrupt opponents and protect its own strategy. The goal is to be as fast and consistent as possible in executing the combo before the opponent can stop it.

The key concept of the deck is to maintain aggressive tempo by disrupting the opponent's plays with cards like Fatal Push, Daze, Thoughtseize, and Force of Will, while also controlling the game with cards like Brainstorm and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. The deck aims to quickly establish board presence with creatures like Brazen Borrower, Barrowgoyf, and Murktide Regent, all while efficiently utilizing tempo plays and disruption to keep the opponent off balance. The deck is built to quickly close out games before the opponent can stabilize.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly and efficiently combo off by milling the entire library, using cards like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer to put cards like Thassa's Oracle into the graveyard. By utilizing fast mana sources like Lotus Petal and Simian Spirit Guide, along with powerful rituals like Cabal Ritual and Dark Ritual, the deck aims to win the game in a single turn. Additionally, cards like Pact of Negation and Cabal Therapy provide disruption and protection to ensure the combo goes off successfully. It is a high-risk, high-reward strategy that seeks to win the game before the opponent can react.

The deck focuses on using fast mana accelerants like Lotus Petal and Lion's Eye Diamond to generate a large storm count and win the game through Tendrils of Agony or Burning Wish for a lethal spell. The deck also utilizes powerful tutors like Infernal Tutor and Beseech the Mirror to search for key combo pieces. With efficient card draw spells like Brainstorm and Duress for disruption, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of spells in a single turn.

The deck focuses on locking down the opponent's strategy by using artifacts to control the board and limit their options. By utilizing powerful artifacts like The One Ring, Karn, the Great Creator, and Trinisphere, the deck is able to create a prison-like environment for the opponent. Additionally, cards like Urza's Tower, Urza's Mine, and Urza's Power Plant provide mana acceleration to cast costly spells like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn. The main goal is to control the game until a win condition can be achieved through either overwhelming the opponent with powerful creatures or forcing them to concede due to the lockdown effect.

The main goal of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's plans while maintaining pressure on the battlefield. By combining aggressive creatures like Barrowgoyf and Murktide Regent with disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Hymn to Tourach, the deck aims to control the tempo of the game. Wasteland and other land destruction cards are used to disrupt the opponent's mana base, while countermagic like Force of Will and Daze provide protection against threats. With a mix of disruption, aggression, and card advantage, this deck seeks to outmaneuver and overwhelm the opponent in Legacy 1 vs 1 matches.

This deck aims to win the game by milling out the opponent's library in a single turn using a combination of cards that allow for a quick and explosive combo. Cards like Undercity Informer, Balustrade Spy, and Summoner's Pact are key pieces in achieving this goal. The deck also includes protection in the form of Force of Will and Pact of Negation to ensure the combo goes off successfully. With the ability to quickly assemble its combo pieces, this deck is designed to win the game before the opponent has a chance to react.

The deck revolves around using powerful artifacts to lock down the opponent's ability to play cards effectively, while using mana ramp to quickly bring out devastating threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Ugin, the Eye of the Storm. With a combination of artifact control pieces like Karn, the Great Creator and Trinisphere, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and overwhelm them with its own powerful spells and abilities. The goal is to establish a dominating board presence and control the game from start to finish.

The Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying aggressive creatures like Amped Raptor and Sand Scout, supported by powerful anthem effects from Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory. It also utilizes disruptive elements like Static Prison and Gaddock Teeg to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while maintaining mana denial with cards like Wasteland and Karakas. The inclusion of efficient removal like Swords to Plowshares helps the deck control the board and maintain pressure on the opponent. Ultimately, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast, efficient threats while disrupting their strategy.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly assemble a combo using cards like Dark Ritual, Lotus Petal, and Simian Spirit Guide to generate mana and cast a large number of spells in a single turn. The goal is to mill the entire deck into the graveyard, triggering cards like Narcomoeba and Bridge from Below, before using Dread Return to reanimate a game-winning creature like Thassa's Oracle. The deck aims to win the game in a single explosive turn, overwhelming the opponent with a flurry of spells and abilities.

The main objective of this deck is to quickly and efficiently combo off by playing a series of spells that manipulate the graveyard and mill the entire library, in order to win the game with Thassa's Oracle. The deck utilizes a combination of fast mana sources, reanimation spells, and powerful creatures to achieve this goal, all while protecting the combo with counter spells and disruption. The deck aims to win the game within the first few turns, by assembling the necessary pieces for a game-ending combo before the opponent has a chance to respond.

This deck revolves around generating lots of mana quickly using cards like Lotus Petal and Lion's Eye Diamond, and then casting a high number of spells in a single turn to build up storm count. The key strategy is to utilize the card Burning Wish to tutor for necessary combo pieces, such as Tendrils of Agony for a lethal finish, or Echo of Eons for a fresh hand of cards. The goal is to storm off and overwhelm the opponent with spells before they can react, achieving victory through powerful combos and efficient card advantage.
